about.title: About PNRM Ukraine
about.description: The Professional Network of Research and Higher Education Managers of Ukraine (PNRM Ukraine) is an independent initiative created to support professionals working in the field of research and education management.
site.title: PROFESSIONAL NETWORK OF RESEARCH AND HE MANAGERS IN UKRAINE
menu.title: Menu
mission.title: Our mission
mission.subtitle: unite managers of higher education and science from different regions of Ukraine in order to effectively cooperate, realize educational and scientific potential, and establish interaction between national and international networks.
dialog.1: More than 60 managers from higher education institutions and scientific institutions representing almost all regions of Ukraine joined the PNRM Network.
dialog.linkText: To become a member of the community, read the {statuteLink} and fill out the {formLink}
dialog.3: After confirmation, the Network member receives confirmation in the form of a certificate.
direction.title: Our directions
direction.subtitle: We invite you to join the team of educators and scientists (regardless of experience and position): who have their own ideas regarding the development of the sphere of education and science; the desire to work to strengthen and realize the educational and scientific potential of the system of higher education and science of Ukraine.

will take place next Friday at 2:00 PM CET (Central European Time).

You can join the meeting using the following credentials: 

Meeting ID: 247 425 832 060

event.title.3:
On May 14, 2024, the event 'Being Visible as a Science Manager' will take place 

Time? 2:00 p.m. - 3:30 p.m. (CET) / 3:00 p.m. - 4:30 p.m. (OET)
eventDetail.title.3: On May 14, 2024, the event 'Being Visible as a Science Manager' will take place
eventDetail.description.3:
'Being Visible as a Science Manager' 

As a science manager, most of your activities are conducted without any visibility. Your results are visible. But what about your own visibility? For what purpose and in front of whom do you want to be visible? 

PNRM-VCi jointly with the Network for Science Management in Germany (NWM) and the Potsdam Graduate School (PoGS) invite you to an interactive digital mini-seminar: 'Being Visible as a Science Manager'. 

When? Tuesday, May 14, 2024 

Time? 2:00 p.m. - 3:30 p.m. (CET) / 3:00 p.m. - 4:30 p.m. (OET) 

Where? Zoom. 

To participate, registration is mandatory here👉 Being Visible as a Science Manager Tickets, Tue, May 14, 2024 at 2:00 p.m. | Eventbrite. 

Limited seats available.
event.title.4:
BAYHOST Workshop: Cooperation with Ukraine in the Humanities. 

Participation in the conference is free.
eventDetail.title.4: BAYHOST Workshop: Cooperation with Ukraine in the Humanities
eventDetail.description.4:
BAYHOST Workshop: Cooperation with Ukraine in the Humanities 

BAYHOST, the German Language Research Center in Central, Eastern, and Southeastern Europe (FZ DiMOS), from the Department of Slavic Philology – Linguistics at the Institute of Slavic Studies at the University of Regensburg, and the Andrássy University Budapest (AUB) are organizing a workshop on cooperation with Ukraine in the humanities from October 24 to 25 in Budapest in a hybrid format.

Participation in the conference is free, and financial assistance for accommodation and travel is possible.

Applications for participation must be submitted by June 30: 

https://www.daad-ukraine.org/uk/2024/06/06/vorkshop-bayhost-spivpratsya-z-ukrayinoyu-v-sferi-gumanitarnih-nauk/
event.title.5: SEPTEMBER 26 AND 27, 2024, AT SAARLAND UNIVERSITY IN SAARBRÜCKEN, THE NETWORK CONFERENCE 'UKRAINIAN DAYS IN SAARLAND' WILL TAKE PLACE
eventDetail.title.5: SEPTEMBER 26 AND 27, 2024, AT SAARLAND UNIVERSITY IN SAARBRÜCKEN, THE NETWORK CONFERENCE 'UKRAINIAN DAYS IN SAARLAND' WILL TAKE PLACE
eventDetail.description.5:
On September 26 and 27, 2024, at Saarland University in Saarbrücken, the network conference 'Ukrainian Days in Saarland' will be held, organized by Professor Andriy Luzhetsky in cooperation with the German-Ukrainian Academic Society. 

The event aims to strengthen academic exchange with Ukraine. The event is funded by DAAD as part of the Go-East program with funds from BMBF. Participation is possible online or on-campus and is free of charge. 

Registration is mandatory on the website 

www.amegbio.com/ukrainian-days-in-saarland 

until 31.08.2024 

Additional information 

https://www.amegbio.com/ukrainian-days-in-saarland 

about the event and the German-Ukrainian Academic Society.
event.title.6: The PNRM Network now has a WhatsApp community! Join to stay updated on all news.
eventDetail.title.6: The PNRM Network now has a WhatsApp community!
eventDetail.description.6:
Due to the ban on Telegram in some institutions at the state level, we are creating a WhatsApp group to stay connected at all times. WhatsApp’s capabilities turned out to be broader than we expected, making it a great opportunity for even more effective communication. Join the community to stay informed, share experiences, and discuss important issues in real time. We’re waiting for you here: 

https://chat.whatsapp.com/JStu2Rvebwe5U1p5HQIR7B
event.title.7: Online Workshop: 'Basic Knowledge of Open Educational Resources (OER)' Participation is free!
eventDetail.title.7: Online Workshop: 'Basic Knowledge of Open Educational Resources (OER)'
eventDetail.description.7:
April 15, 2025

15:00–16:30 (Kyiv time)

Online | Working language: English

Participation is free

How to effectively use Open Educational Resources (OER) in teaching, research, and creating your own content?

We invite educators, staff from higher education institutions, students, and anyone interested to join the practical workshop led by experts from TIB – Leibniz Information Centre for Science and Technology.

During the event, participants will learn:
What OER is and why it matters for education and science;
How copyright and open licenses work;
How to use the platform twillo.de https://www.twillo.de/
How to publish your own educational materials in compliance with legal norms.

To participate, please send an email to: info@twillo.de

Join us to open up new educational opportunities!

More details here: https://www.twillo.de/oer/web/workshop-oer-basics-and-fundamentals-for-higher-education/
team.member.position.1: doctor of biological sciences, professor expert in the field: science organizations, project management, doctoral schools
team.member.description.1: «Today, every Ukrainian defends our country on his own front. During the full-scale invasion of Russia on the territory of Ukraine, we became even more united, felt our integrity and strength, as well as the support of the entire democratic world. To realize the educational and scientific potential without limitations and borders, active educators and scientists united and created the first Ukrainian-European community Professional Network of Managers of Education and Science of Ukraine».
team.member.position.2: expert on grant support, project implementation
team.member.description.2: «At this time, it is important for the country to support and develop every sphere, but by working on the development of education, science and innovation, we have the opportunity to plan the future of our country, to prepare a powerful personnel potential».
team.member.position.3: communication expert in the field of education, science and innovation.
team.member.description.3: «For the formation of educators and scientists, it is important to establish professional communications, which will become an important basis for the exchange of experience, cooperation for the development and formation of the sphere of education, science and innovation. This, in turn, will become an invaluable weapon of our front».
team.member.position.4: expert in educational process organization and reputation management
team.member.description.4: «There is no future without quality education. We live in difficult times. No one believed that a neighbor could attack us. However, we courageously hold the educational front. And we teach qualitatively and professionally. War is scary, but you need to move forward and develop to rebuild the country and return to a peaceful life. With joint efforts, we will rebuild and develop Ukraine, because it is our native land. I invite you to join our team and move forward to Victory together».
team.member.position.5: expert on science management and funding, interaction between science and business, innovation development and technology transfer
team.member.description.5: «Ukraine demonstrates its courage every day and heroically opposes Russian aggression, works tirelessly to overcome the influence of the Russian aggression, and thus proves its strength and power to the world every day. Undoubtedly Victory is ours, but unfortunately we have many destructions and losses that need to be restored as soon as possible. and Today's realities also require new mechanisms for the development of our country, namely innovative, high-tech and investment-attractive directions of the state. We have an incredible intellectual, scientific and innovative potential that requires support, coordinated actions and interaction of government bodies, the scientific community and business, and the single Network in Ukraine opens up such opportunities».
team.member.description.6: «Today requires modern and quick solutions. That is why a network was created, which will unite a significant intellectual potential for the reconstruction of Ukraine in an innovative way».
team.member.position.7: expert on grant activities, project management
team.member.description.7: «The professional activity of almost every educator and scientist is a project activity. The peculiarity of projects is that they have a beginning and an end, they have tasks, and resources for their implementation. Having an idea and the ability to effectively implement projects, especially in conditions of uncertainty, is our special superpower. The network makes it possible to increase the level of superpower, and therefore we are all superheroes of the educational and scientific front».
team.member.description.8: «For the development of our country, its scientific and educational potential, we must work systematically, not situationally. The network creates opportunities for the joint search for new approaches, ideas, solutions, for the exchange of experience and best practices at the national and international levels».
team.member.description.9: «Modernity, clarity and innovation dictate trends. The network of managers opens up new opportunities for the development of the country, shows attention to details, professionalism and balance, which is reflected in the visual part of the network».
team.member.description.10: «Time is a valuable resource that is always in short supply. This network is, in a way, a platform for quick search for cooperation and interaction and communication between active scientists and educators».

news.item20: EUComplianceM4UA: The Third Transnational Project Meeting Confirmed the Practical Value of the Project for Ukrainian Universities
newsDetail.news20.title: EUComplianceM4UA: The Third Transnational Project Meeting Confirmed the Practical Value of the Project for Ukrainian Universities
newsDetail.news20.description:
In March 2026, the third transnational meeting of the EUComplianceM4UA project (TPM3) took place. Over three days – from March 17 to 19 – participants worked online, hosted by the Central Ukrainian National Technical University.

From PNRM Ukraine, as an associated partner, Chair of the Board Viktoriia Hura joined the event. In her welcome speech, she emphasized the importance of developing a compliance culture in higher education and spreading best practices of integrity-based governance. She also noted that the project already has a tangible impact: in particular, it strengthened the methodological foundation and content of the All-Ukrainian Training for Science Managers held in December 2025, supporting the professional development of administrators and specialists engaged in internationalization and quality assurance in universities.

Meeting Highlights

Day 1 – Opening & Quality Review

The first day focused on the opening session, reviewing project progress, and discussing key management issues. Participants examined the current status of work packages, set priorities for the next period, and reviewed quality assurance recommendations already obtained during implementation.

Day 2 – Partner Presentations & WP4

The second day was dedicated to presentations from Ukrainian universities. Discussions centered on the state of project implementation at institutional level, progress under WP4, and the launch of educational activities – forming study groups, starting teaching, organizing learning processes, and ensuring methodological support.

Day 3 – Dissemination, KPI & Sustainability

The final day focused on dissemination and communication, aligning key performance indicators (KPI), and planning further outreach. Special attention was given to sustainability plans and coordination of next steps for the upcoming period.

Project Results and Tools

The project has already produced resources of practical value for universities, educators, students, and business stakeholders.

The interactive bilingual FutureBook on compliance management is available online: https://vscht.futurebooks.cz/kategorie/25-eucompliancem4ua

The open EUComplianceM4UA YouTube channel provides promotional and educational videos: https://www.youtube.com/@eucompliancem4ua

Looking Ahead

Partners confirmed a shared vision of compliance education as a sustainable direction in universities. This includes expanding learning opportunities, strengthening interdisciplinarity, improving methodological support, and enhancing cooperation with business partners. A particular emphasis was placed on systematic analysis of project impact – from tracking engagement and learning outcomes to assessing how new tools, policies, and communication products transform institutional practices and foster a culture of integrity and responsible governance.
newsDetail.news19.title: PNRM held the first training for Ukraine’s science managers: from dream to plan
newsDetail.news18.title: PNRM Ukraine invites you to the Research Manangers & Administrators
newsDetail.news18.description:
We are pleased to invite you to the III Annual Meeting of PNRM Ukraine members and partners and to the Training for Research Managers and Administrators, which will take place on 📅 December 4–5, 2025 at Taras Shevchenko National University of Kyiv, Ukraine.

About the Event

This year, we are combining our traditional annual network meeting with a national training event, which we believe will become a landmark occasion in the field of science and higher education.
Organizers: Taras Shevchenko National University of Kyiv in collaboration with PNRM Ukraine, supported by the Ministry of Education and Science of Ukraine.

Participation Format
• Training (December 4–5): in-person only
• III Annual PNRM Meeting (December 5): hybrid format (offline in Kyiv and online for participants from other regions), open to PNRM Ukraine members

The training program is attached to this letter. The agenda for the annual meeting will be shared later.
All training participants will receive a certificate of professional development with a supplement outlining acquired competencies.

Please note: registration for the training and the annual meeting is separate.

Registration
• For the Annual PNRM Meeting (online or offline): https://forms.gle/4wdGtTuHy183KVZV9
• For the Training (in-person only): https://forms.gle/ox2au5jfRJozhtjR6

Participation Fee (Training, December 4–5)

Covers coffee breaks, lunch, and organizational support:
• PNRM Ukraine members — 400 UAH
• Other participants — 600 UAH

Payment details:
Recipient: NGO PMMON UKRAINE
EDRPOU Code: 44723370
IBAN: UA743052990000026000006231995
Bank: JSC CB "PRIVATBANK"
Payment reference: Participation fee [Full Name] for "All-Ukrainian Training for Science Managers and Administrators"

Accommodation

Taras Shevchenko National University of Kyiv offers accommodation at the hotel complex of the Institute of Continuing Education (ICE), located at 36 Vasylkivska St. (5-minute walk from "Vasylkivska" metro station).

For accommodation inquiries, please contact:
Olena Pochaievets — +38 066 587 04 12, olena.pochaievets@knu.ua

We look forward to seeing you in Kyiv for an event that brings together professional growth, experience exchange, and the inspiration of the PNRM community!
Event program available here: [[pdf:/img/program2025.pdf]]
newsDetail.news17.title: Invitation to Participate in the International RAAAP-4 Survey
newsDetail.news17.description:
PNRM Ukraine, as a member of the global INORMS network, invites you to take part in the fourth international RAAAP-4 survey — a large-scale research project that provides an up-to-date picture of the research management profession worldwide.

This study continues to build a consistent dataset about our professional community, helping to deepen understanding of the needs, challenges, and prospects in the field of research management.

Why Your Participation Matters

By taking part in this survey, you help:
• shape recommendations for training and professional development;
• support effective recruitment and retention strategies;
• amplify the voice of Ukrainian professionals at the international level.

Who Is Behind the Survey?

The survey is authorized and supported by INORMS — the International Network of Research Management Societies, of which PNRM Ukraine is a member. It was developed by the INORMS RAAAP Working Group, which includes more than 30 experts from various countries, including:

• Simon Kerridge (University of Kent, UK)
• Madhuri Dutta (George Institute for Global Health, India)
• Melinda Fischer (Clemson University, USA)
• Cristina Oliveira (NOVA School of Information Management, Portugal)
• José Santos (Polytechnic Institute of Bragança, Portugal)

How to Participate

• ⏱️ Estimated time: approximately 20–30 minutes
• 🔗 Survey link: access the form: https://clemson.ca1.qualtrics.com/jfe/form/SV_5uyhxRCNEA7i7r0

Please note: the survey must not be forwarded to Russia, Iran, or North Korea without special authorization, in accordance with international sanctions.

Learn More
• Project page on the INORMS website: https://inorms.net/activities/raaap-taskforce/
• Official RAAAP website: https://sites.google.com/view/raaap
newsDetail.news16.title: PNRM Representatives at the 35th Annual EAIE Conference in Gothenburg
newsDetail.news16.description:
For the third year in a row, PNRM Ukraine has held an honorable place at the Study in Ukraine booth.
On 9–12 September 2025, the 35th Annual Conference and Exhibition of the European Association for International Education (EAIE) — one of the largest events in the field of higher education internationalization — took place, with PNRM members participating. This year, PNRM was represented by its active members:
📌Maryna Voloshchuk (Kamianets-Podilskyi State Institute) took part in the poster session, presenting the results of her research “Inclusion: Socialisation via Internationalisation”. The presentation sparked a lively discussion on current issues of inclusion, internationalization, and expanding opportunities for young people in modern society, with contributions from representatives of universities in Belgium, Sweden, Austria, the Netherlands, Germany, Poland, and other countries.
In addition, Tetiana Maiboroda, Iryna Shteimiller, and Maryna Voloshchuk, as active members of the European Association for International Education, took part in discussions on various international platforms. They drew the attention of the global academic community to the key challenges and problems of Ukrainian education, science, and society as a whole.
newsDetail.news15.title: REGISTRATION OPEN FOR THE MINT-UKRAINE PROGRAM: MICRO-CREDENTIALS AS AN INTERNATIONALISATION TOOL FOR UKRAINIAN UNIVERSITIES
newsDetail.news15.description:
PNRM Ukraine invites representatives of Ukrainian higher education institutions to join the blended learning program dedicated to the development of micro-credentials as a tool for internationalisation.
📅 Application deadline — 11 September 2025
🔗 Registration form and participation details: https://www.uni-hannover.de/en/universitaet/internationales/cctonmoi/mint-ukraine#c175099

The MInT-Ukraine program offers an opportunity to:

— Learn about European practices of micro-credentials
— Develop an institutional internationalisation strategy
— Build partnerships with international organisations

Organizer: International Office LUH
Supported by: DAAD and BMBF within the German-Ukrainian University Network program

Partners:
— Ukrainian State Center for International Education (Study in Ukraine)
— Professional Network of Science and Education Managers of Ukraine (PNRM Ukraine)
— German-Ukrainian Academic Society (DUAG)
— National Erasmus+ Office Ukraine
— EAIE: European Association for International Education

Participation in the program is a step towards strengthening the international position of Ukrainian universities and developing new educational formats.
newsDetail.news14.title: Participation of PNRM Ukraine in the first kick-off TPM of the #EUComplianceM4UA project
newsDetail.news14.description:
On January 20-21, 2025, representatives of PNRM Ukraine participated in the first kick-off TPM (Transnational Project Meeting) within the framework of the #EUComplianceM4UA project 'Implementation of the European approach of Compliance Management in higher education in Ukraine,' where PNRM acts as an associated partner to disseminate the project results and best practices.

The kick-off meeting was attended by the founder of PNRM Ukraine, Anna Tolstanova, and the current chair of the board, Viktoria Gura. Anna Tolstanova delivered a welcoming speech, sharing the history, opportunities, and prospects of the organization's work.

During the two-day kick-off meeting, project management issues, draft organizational documents in the field of management, dissemination, and quality assurance were discussed. The concept of training for Ukrainian partners and the development of educational content in the field of compliance management were also reviewed.
The main coordinator of the project is Hochschule Wismar, University of Applied Sciences: Technology, Business and Design.

European partners include:
Universidade Portucalense Infante D Henrique-Cooperativa de Ensino Superior Crl (UPT)
VŠCHT Praha Vysoka Skola Chemico-Technologika v Praze (VSCHT) / The University of Chemistry and Technology, Prague
Wyższa Szkoła Przedsiębiorczości i Administracji w Lublinie (WSPA) / The University College of Enterprise and Administration in Lublin

Among Ukrainian universities, the following are participating in the project:
Central Ukrainian National Technical University (CUNTU)
Izmail State University of Humanities (ISUH)
Simon Kuznets Kharkiv National University of Economics (S.KUZNETS KhNUE)
Donetsk State University of Internal Affairs (DonSUIA)
V. N. Karazin Kharkiv National University
newsDetail.news13.title: COLLABORATION, INNOVATION, AND LEADERSHIP: ANNUAL MEETING OF THE PROFESSIONAL NETWORK OF SCIENCE AND EDUCATION MANAGERS OF UKRAINE
newsDetail.news13.description:
On October 21-22, 2024, the third annual meeting of representatives, members, and partners of the Professional Network of Science and Education Managers of Ukraine (PNRM Ukraine) took place. The event, held in a hybrid format, gathered numerous specialists and partners from across Ukraine to share experiences, exchange knowledge, and develop new ideas in the field of science and education management.

The meeting included the annual assembly of PNRM members, focus groups for the project ‘Career Development Roadmap for Women Leaders in Higher Education in Ukraine,’ implemented under the British Council’s Going Global Partnerships gender equality initiative, and the third International Forum ‘Internationalization of Ukrainian Higher Education Institutions: German-Ukrainian Experience.’ This project is part of a professional development program for administrators of Ukrainian higher education institutions in education and academic management, financially supported by the German Academic Exchange Service (DAAD) and the German Federal Ministry of Education and Research (BMBF).

Over the two days, participants actively engaged, shared experiences, and developed new ideas. The network expanded, inspiring members to achieve new milestones, showing that managers are also talented individuals capable of bringing their ideas to life! (link to manager blog)

Participants had the opportunity to join discussions, debates, and workshops on topics of collaboration, innovative development, and leadership in higher education. The hybrid format allowed more managers to join the event and actively participate in the sessions.

The meeting of the Professional Network of Science and Education Managers of Ukraine (PNRM Ukraine) marked another step in strengthening cooperation and knowledge exchange among education and science managers. It reaffirmed that through collaborative efforts and experience sharing, significant results can be achieved, advancing educational and scientific projects in Ukraine to a high standard. The network continues to grow and inspire its members toward new achievements and successes!
newsDetail.news12.title: PNRM Ukraine and the German Network of Science Management (Netzwerk Wissenschaftsmanagement) exchanged experiences
newsDetail.news12.description:
On May 14, 2024, PNRM Ukraine, together with the Network of Science Management in Germany (NWM), met within the framework of an interactive digital workshop titled "Being Visible as a Science Manager." It was organized by the Potsdam Graduate School (PoGS) and the International Office of Hannover University for network representatives as part of the DAAD project "Management of Internationalisation and German-Ukrainian Academic Cooperation 2024" https://www.uni-hannover.de/en/universitaet/internationales/moi-ukraine 

We visited digital rooms where we exchanged experiences about our visibility as science managers. We identified what motivates us to actively work on enhancing our visibility. 

We then opened rooms with current topics. Rooms from PNRM were created by Oksana Berezhna, Konstantin Kyrychenko, and Natalia Bovtruk. 

Oksana Berezhna is the General Director of the Directorate of Innovation and Science Relations with the Real Sector of the Economy at the Ministry of Education and Science of Ukraine, National Coordinator of COST in Ukraine, and Co-founder of the NGO "Professional Network of Education and Science Managers of Ukraine." She is the author of projects aimed at fostering collaboration between business and science, enhancing technology transfer, and commercializing scientific results, including the web platform "Science and Business," the project "Science and Business" by the Ministry of Education and the Ukrainian Startup Fund, Info Science Bot - a Telegram bot for quick information for Ukrainians, and Science&Business Online Mentor. 

Konstantin Kyrychenko is the Assistant Rector for International Relations at Sumy State University, Head of the International Affairs Department, PhD in Technical Sciences, Associate Professor, Erasmus+ Coordinator, Twinning Coordinator, and Member of the Supervisory Board of the Alliance of Ukrainian Universities. 

Nataliya Bovtruk is a PhD in Pedagogical Sciences, an expert in the field of education, science, and innovation at PNRM Ukraine, and the Communications Manager of the NGO "Trinity Hub." 

From the German side, the speakers included: 

- Alice Hon, Administrative Director of the Leibniz Institute for Agricultural Engineering and Bioeconomy (ATB), Potsdam, and Chair of the Administration of the Helmholtz Institute for RNA-based Infection Research in Würzburg. 
- Lea Lang, Manager for Scientific Careers and Talents at the Helmholtz Association (delegated coordinator of DESY Diversity and Inclusion) and Lecturer at Humboldt University of Berlin. 
- Gerrit Ressler, Program Manager, International Relations, and Scientific Communication, Department of Higher Education and Research, Health and Long-Term Care, Berlin, and Senior Consultant of the german.innovation program. 

What did they talk about? Of course, about science and communication. Nataliya Bovtruk also realized during the second interactive room that she would invite people to her room to discuss a very important topic for Ukraine today. She talked about a new project in Ukraine - a unique educational and rehabilitation center for visually impaired individuals called "Trinity Hub." Nataliya called for cooperation among education and science managers who are researching issues related to visual impairments. 

More information about the project can be found here: 

- Website: https://trinityhub.org.ua/ 
- Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/trinity.hub.ua 
- YouTube: https://www.youtube.com/@Trinity_hub2023 
- TikTok: https://www.tiktok.com/@trinity.hub.ua 

In conclusion, apart from motivation, positive impressions, and inspiration for new projects, everyone discovered something new. This was the main goal of the event, and we look forward to the next interesting events.
newsDetail.news1.title: PNRM participated in the 3rd project seminar Course: internationalization management and German-Ukrainian academic cooperation 2022-2023
newsDetail.news1.description:
Today, June 2, 2023, the 3rd project seminar "Course: internationalization management and German-Ukrainian academic cooperation 2022-2023" was held on the basis of Taras Shevchenko Kyiv National University.

During the event, PNRM members presented the work of the Network, plans and prospects, as well as the platform, which will be operational in the near future.

Members of the Network held a general meeting to accept the results, reports, elect the supervisory board, as well as the plan for the next year.

To all those who were unable to join, we remind you that you are members of the Professional Network of Managers of Education and Science of Ukraine, the main purpose of which is to unite managers of higher education and science from different regions of Ukraine for the purpose of effective cooperation, realization of educational and scientific potential, establishment of interaction between national and international networks.

Therefore, we draw your attention to the fact that inactive members of the Network will be submitted to the consideration of the general meeting, as well as the newly created supervisory board for the deprivation of membership.

We are grateful to those who believe in our work and actively participate. It is us today who are changing the culture of project implementation in the field of education, science and innovation in Ukraine, as well as popularizing our activities in the world.

Thank you for being with us! Let's hold on! Everything will be Ukraine! 💙

Join and talk about us on social networks, https://www.linkedin.com/company/pnrm-ukraine, https://twitter.com/pnrm_ukraine, or write to the email address: pnrm.ukraine@gmail.com.

For your convenience, the Network's Telegram channel https://t.me/+YxvybUYgwGFhNzky and members' chat https://t.me/+J1cr33Ae8pA3ZGMy are open.

If you have not yet received a newsletter from the Network, fill out the form https://forms.gle/YCkTXopGUCJjgSfz9.
newsDetail.news2.title: PNRM began to form directions
newsDetail.news2.description:
A very difficult year for each of us is coming to an end. However, despite everything, we have many good events this year, which became important years for the formation of the sphere of education and science, and therefore Ukraine.

It was this year that the Professional Network of Education and Science Managers of Ukraine began to form. Our main goal is to create a strong network of professionals in the field of education and science.

The network of managers has already begun to form in the following directions:

1. Development of personal competencies of the manager
2. Grant activity and international network
3. Quality of higher education
4. Innovations and technology transfer
5. Popularization of science

PNRM started cooperation with the participants of the MolUkr project and the regional group Berlin Branderburg Netzwerk Wissenschaftsmanagement. Joint webinars were jointly organized and held within the Virtual NWM Academy.

In September, PNRM took part in the 32nd annual conference of the European Association for International Education. During the event, the sphere of higher education of Ukraine was awarded the "EAIE-2022" award in the "Vision and Leadership" category from the European Association of International Education (EAIE). The winner of the prize for vision and leadership for the first time in 32 years was not a separate organization, but the sphere of higher education of Ukraine as a whole for its reaction and resilience during Russia's brutal war against Ukraine.

Yes, we have to admit that this is not all that we planned. But we will not stop and will continue to form.

We sincerely congratulate you on the upcoming holidays! May everyone have healthy children and parents. Despite all the obstacles, so that everyone has prosperity, and also realizes all ideas and ideas.

Happy New Year!
Merry Christmas!

Everything will be Ukraine!
newsDetail.news3.title: The network now has a PNRM channel
newsDetail.news3.description:
On December 16, 2022, the Strategic Session of the Professional Network of Managers of Education and Science of Ukraine was held online. Important issues were discussed in order to continue forming a strong network of professionals in the field of education and science.

We have another news. In order to have maximum communication with us, we are creating a PNRM channel. To join, follow the link https://t.me/PNRMchannel.
newsDetail.news4.title: PNRM suggests reading the report poll
newsDetail.news4.description:
PNRM suggests reading the survey report on support measures for those Ukrainian researchers who left Ukraine after the Russian invasion on February 24, 2022.

The results are offered as empirical evidence for the planning of support programs by academic and financial institutions. It can also be useful when conducting research. In particular, information is presented here about the demographic profile, discipline and career stage of scientists in need, as well as about the special professional and life problems they face.

The survey is based on 619 responses provided by the Ukrainian research community during June-September 2022.

More details: https://bit.ly/3FrR3JK.
newsDetail.news5.title: The PNRM offices suffered from massive Russian missile attacks
newsDetail.news5.description:
On October 10, the buildings of Taras Shevchenko National University of Kyiv were damaged by massive Russian missile strikes. This place gave rise to the creation of the Professional Network of Education and Science Managers of Ukraine. The office of the Ministry of Education and Science of Ukraine was damaged, and most of all, the offices of the Directorate of Science and Innovation. Experts in this unit are part of the Network. Also, the office of the National Antarctic Science Center was damaged.

No one was injured among the experts, educators, and scientists. But the powerful blast wave knocked out many specified offices and damaged office equipment, personal belongings, etc.

With these photos https://bit.ly/3VuSm1r , and messages, we want to inform the educational and scientific community about yet another proof of Russian war crimes.

Now we are all recovering and continuing to work for the sake of the future. Keep yourselves!
newsDetail.news6.title: The first session of the Virtual Academy of Science Management was held
newsDetail.news6.description:
The first session of the Virtual Academy of Science Management was held by the Professional Network of Education and Science Managers of Ukraine (PNRM Ukraine) together with Netzwerk Wissenschaftsmanagement (NWM Germany).

The topic of the event was devoted to building an effective network for science management. Educators and scientists from Ukraine, Germany and other countries will discuss issues for establishing educational and scientific communication. The participants were also able to exchange experience and tasks for the formation of an international network of managers of education and science.
newsDetail.news7.title: PNRM took part in the 32nd annual conference of the European Association of International Education
newsDetail.news7.description:
The 32nd annual conference of the European Association for International Education (EAIE), which took place on September 13-16, 2022 in the city of Barcelona (Kingdom of Spain), has ended. The Ukrainian delegation, which included representatives of the Professional Network of Managers of Education and Science of Ukraine, took part in the event.

During the event, the sphere of higher education of Ukraine was awarded the "EAIE-2022" award in the "Vision and Leadership" category from the European Association of International Education (EAIE). The winner of the prize for vision and leadership for the first time in 32 years was not a separate organization, but the sphere of higher education of Ukraine as a whole for its reaction and resilience during Russia's brutal war against Ukraine.

"Today, every Ukrainian defends our country on his own front. During the full-scale invasion of Russia on the territory of Ukraine, we became even more united, felt our integrity and strength, as well as the support of the entire democratic world. Thus, the impetus for the creation of the Professional Network of Education and Science Managers of Ukraine, which is the first Ukrainian-European community for the realization of educational and scientific potential without limitations and borders, was the support of German colleagues within the framework of the DAAD MoI Ukraine (Management of Internationalization and German-Ukrainian Academic Cooperation)," said PNRM co-founder Hanna Tolstanova.

A total of 213 stands were presented at the conference, which was visited by 6,000 people. More than 500 representatives of universities, research institutions and companies providing educational services visited the Ukrainian stand.

Within the framework of the conference, the session "What's new in Ukraine" was held, devoted to an overview of the current situation, news in the field of higher education, science and innovation in Ukraine, stability in the conditions of the brutal war of the Russian Federation against Ukraine, challenges of displaced universities, the impact of war on scientists and innovators, opportunities and development of international cooperation. During the session, information about PNRM was presented with the aim of expanding the network's international cooperation and informing European colleagues about the introduced initiative, vision and areas of activity.

PNRM representatives held a number of meetings with representatives of European universities, research institutions and companies providing educational services, enlisted their support and discussed possible joint projects. Negotiations were also held regarding joint trainings for the professionalization of higher education managers of Ukraine with EAIE, the Ministry of Science and Education of Catalonia.
newsDetail.news8.title: How do educators protect the educational front during the war?
newsDetail.news8.description:
How do educators protect the educational front during the war? The answer to this important question was given to the "Svit" newspaper during an interview by the vice-rector for scientific work of Taras Shevchenko Kyiv National University, professor Hanna Tolstanova.

In addition, she talked about studying in wartime conditions, working in new realities, about new strategically important steps for the opportunities of Ukrainian scientists to more closely enter the European research space, gain new knowledge, continue their research, etc.

As Hanna Tolstanova noted, the best effective tool for Ukraine will be a professional network, an informal platform where you can discuss problems, initiatives, projects, and simply exchange ideas.

"We are all managers of science and education - we do not compete with each other. On the contrary, our task is to be such high-level professionals as to create the best service for scientists, for the development of science. And we created such a professional network of higher education science managers. Also, we are already supported by a similar German association and provides advisory assistance," the vice-rector emphasized.

We will remind you that an informal meeting was held recently, at which 36 universities from different regions of Ukraine were represented. The skeleton was formed - 20 of the most proactive, those who want to work, have ideas. There are many initiatives, so we invite you to join and develop the Network together.
newsDetail.news9.title: PNRM held a joint meeting with German colleagues
newsDetail.news9.description: On June 24, 2022, the founders of the Professional Network of Education and Science Managers of Ukraine (PNRM) held a joint meeting with the participants of the MolUkr project and the Berlin Brandenburg regional group Netzwerk Wissenschaftsmanagement. During the online meeting, PNRM, a strategic vision for future cooperation, as well as the results of PNRM's activities were presented. At the end of the event it was agreed to organize and hold a joint virtual round table for capacity building within the Virtual NWM Academy.
newsDetail.news10.title: PNRM is officially registered in the Unified State Register
newsDetail.news10.description:
The Professional Network of Education and Science Managers of Ukraine (PNRM) is officially registered in the Unified State Register. Yesterday, June 15, 2022, another meeting of the founders of the organization was held. Note that our main goal is to create a strong network of professionals in the field of education and science.

The network of managers has already begun to form in the following directions:

1. Development of personal competencies of the manager
2. Grant activity and international network
3. Quality of higher education
4. Innovations and technology transfer
5. Popularization of science
newsDetail.news11.title: The founding meeting of the founders of PNRM was held
newsDetail.news11.description: There was a meeting of the founders of the Professional Network of Education and Science Managers of Ukraine. Who are the managers of education and science? They are competent specialists who will help scientists and educators to implement innovative approaches to the development of educational, scientific, technical and innovative activities, as well as the promotion of science and the establishment of international relations. And all these activities will be in Ukraine and abroad.
universities.polissia.name: Polissia National University
universities.karazin.name: V. N. Karazin Kharkiv National University
blog.title: There will be stories of our members, as well as of all those who are willing.
blog.subtitle: This blog is a space for the voices of Ukrainian scientists, educators, and researchers. We share stories of invisible work, discoveries, challenges, and inspiration from those who shape our future. Would you like to share your experience? Write to us — we’ll help you shape your story with care and dignity fill out the form
blog.post.1.title: GALAXY PNRM
blog.post.1.content:
Communication and cooperation — perhaps grace, perhaps leadership. Here everything merges in synergy, here true energy is felt! Our path is enriched with ideas and growth; every step matters, and mistakes do not exist. Interaction, partnership, strategy — this is our special galaxy. This is professional, this is about management: we find balance and solutions at any moment! Higher education and innovation are steps toward success and motivation. We shape values, build connections — crazy risks? We minimize them! Our ambition is to find content where the search for something new feels like the fifth element. Share your experience, discover a new world — the network is created, may success lift us high! A community of leaders, an educational climate within us, strength in unity — don’t miss your time!

✅ PARTNERSHIP PNRM — a powerful tandem, bold decisions — let’s begin! A boundless space for ideas, priceless experience for people! For those who can and know how, a team of action — an open path! Program implementation: a new approach — you know it well. Among them, Erasmus — an exchange program that means possible training or study, scholarships and internships. Support for the growth of those who strive for more or are starting for the first time, and hoping for a grant! So find your Horizon, the challenging research front. Unite! Believe in Victory! Your dialogue will find its way! Partnership is always about us, together — we are strength! At a good time!
blog.post.1.author: Iryna Honcharenko, member of PNRM

euCompliance.funding.title: EU Funding Programme
euCompliance.funding.instrument: Neighbourhood, Development and International Cooperation Instrument
euCompliance.funding.agreement: Framework Agreement between the Government of Ukraine and the Commission of the European Communities, ratified by the Verkhovna Rada of Ukraine with a statement by Law of 03.09.2008 № 360-VI (360-17) - registered project of EU International Technical Assistance to Ukraine
euCompliance.funding.amount.label: Erasmus+ (CBHE) grant amount
euCompliance.targetGroups.title: Target groups
euCompliance.targetGroups.group1.title: Teaching staff of higher education institutions
euCompliance.targetGroups.group1.descrip: The project will provide training and webinars to learn about European approaches to compliance management, which will enhance their qualifications and knowledge level, and contribute to the professional development of university teachers through access to relevant and advanced knowledge in this area. The strengthening of the methodological basis for compliance management provided by the project will contribute to the creation of more modern and relevant educational programs and teaching methods.
euCompliance.targetGroups.group2.title: Master's students
euCompliance.targetGroups.group2.descrip: Students will have the opportunity to acquire new, relevant knowledge and practical skills in compliance management, which will improve their prospects in the labor market and career development opportunities in a demanding business environment after graduation. The implementation of the European approach will provide students with the opportunity to study and use international experience in the field of compliance management, which will expand their global outlook taking into account the characteristics of the common labor market and legislation.
euCompliance.targetGroups.group3.title: Managers and business owners who will receive advanced training and modern knowledge in the field of compliance management
euCompliance.targetGroups.group3.descrip: Relevant knowledge about innovative compliance management practices will allow managers and business owners to improve their management strategies and increase the efficiency of business processes. The knowledge and practical skills acquired during the training will contribute to the implementation and optimization of compliance systems in their own companies, which will increase their efficiency and competitiveness in national and foreign markets, ensure the compliance of the business with international standards and regulatory norms, which, in turn, will contribute to attracting customers and investors.

euCompliance.goals.title: Project goals and objectives
euCompliance.goals.description: The project focuses on implementing the European approach to compliance management with the strategic goal of increasing the level of academic integrity, international management, entrepreneurship, business ethics and legal aspects in less experienced Ukrainian HEIs. The goal of the project is to improve teaching and assessment mechanisms in higher education institutions with a focus on quality assurance, management, anti-corruption, innovation in accordance with EU standards in the field of compliance.
euCompliance.tasks.title: Project tasks
euCompliance.tasks.task1: Improvement of teaching methods. The project aims to improve teaching and assessment mechanisms, quality assurance, management and innovation in Ukrainian HEIs by integrating European compliance methodology.
euCompliance.tasks.task2: Increasing accessibility of education in the field of compliance. Increasing the accessibility and quality of education in compliance management in the field of entrepreneurship, management and law in Ukrainian HEIs to promote adaptation to the requirements of the European labor market.
euCompliance.tasks.task3: Promoting stable employment of graduates of Ukrainian universities. To ensure stable employment opportunities for graduates of Ukrainian universities in the context of European integration and globalization with the help of compliance competencies.
euCompliance.tasks.task4: Promoting digital transformation. To promote digital transformation in teaching compliance management in Ukrainian partner HEIs through innovative tools such as e-books, podcasts, videos and gamification solutions.
euCompliance.activities.title: Activity areas of the project
euCompliance.activities.activity1.title: 1. Improving the capabilities and capacity of academic staff regarding teaching and research in the field of compliance management
euCompliance.activities.activity1.item1: Updating EU content for professional development of Ukrainian HEI teachers in accordance with EU experience and Ukrainian conditions
euCompliance.activities.activity1.item2: Development and implementation of an EU training program on the topic "Introduction to Compliance Management Education in the EU: Innovations and Interdisciplinary Approaches"
euCompliance.activities.activity1.item3: Conducting 4 online webinars (8 days in total) on compliance management (management, economics, entrepreneurship, law and business ethics aspects)
euCompliance.activities.activity1.item4: Development of 4 training programs and activities within the framework of the EU summer school "EUComplianceM4UAmethods", dedicated to EU teaching methods in the field of compliance management
euCompliance.activities.activity1.item5: Conducting the EU summer school "EUComplianceM4UAmethods" on EU teaching methodology in the field of compliance management (management, economics, entrepreneurship, law and business ethics aspects)
euCompliance.activities.activity1.item6: Organization of the final international project conference
euCompliance.activities.activity2.title: 2. Development of 5 courses for master's students of Ukrainian HEIs to improve the level of competencies, skills and employability potential, as well as a manual for master's students "Compliance Management"
euCompliance.activities.activity3.title: 3. Creation of an open micro-certified short-term program on compliance management for business managers in order to ensure better compliance with labor market needs
euCompliance.activities.activity4.title: 4. Promoting digital transformation in teaching compliance management in Ukraine (partner higher education institutions)
euCompliance.activities.activity4.item1: Creation of an introductory training video to demonstrate European experience in the field of compliance management
euCompliance.activities.activity4.item2: Creation of EUComplianceM4UA channel on YouTube
euCompliance.activities.activity4.item3: Development of Futurebook (e-book) "Compliance Management" for master's students (in English and Ukrainian)
euCompliance.activities.activity4.item5: Development of educational podcasts about compliance in Ukraine
euCompliance.results.title: Expected results
euCompliance.results.result1.title: 1. Developed potential of academic staff in the field of compliance management: teaching, training and research
euCompliance.results.result1.item1: EU training program "Introduction to Compliance Management Education: Innovative and Interdisciplinary Approaches"; a total of 28 representatives of HEIs from the EU and Ukraine will be trained
euCompliance.results.result1.item2: 4 online webinars (8 days in total) on compliance management; 25 representatives of Ukrainian HEIs will be trained
euCompliance.results.result1.item3: EU summer school "EUComplianceM4UAmethods" on EU teaching methods in the field of compliance management; 33 representatives of partner HEIs will be trained
euCompliance.results.result1.item4: Final international project conference; a total of 28 representatives of HEIs from the EU and Ukraine will participate
euCompliance.results.result1.item5: 161 internal certificates will be issued after all training and educational activities
euCompliance.results.result1.item6: Adoption of 1 proposal on the implementation of compliance management approaches in Ukrainian higher education
euCompliance.results.result2.title: 2. Development of innovative courses on compliance management for master's students
euCompliance.results.result2.item1: 5 syllabi (in English and Ukrainian) for courses
euCompliance.results.result2.item2: 5 courses for master's students, including training materials, digital video and e-learning content
euCompliance.results.result2.item4: Approximately 150 students at the master's level will be trained
euCompliance.results.result3.title: 3. Certified professional development in the field of compliance management
euCompliance.results.result3.item1: One open micro-certified short-term program on compliance management for business managers, including training materials, digital video and e-learning content
euCompliance.results.result3.item2: About 100 business managers will be trained under this program
euCompliance.results.result4.title: 4. Digital resources and multimedia tools for learning compliance management
euCompliance.results.result4.item1: Introductory training video to demonstrate European experience in the field of compliance management

projects.page.title: Our Projects
projects.page.description: Explore our current and completed projects
INORMS.title: INORMS Partnership
INORMS.subtitle: PNRM Ukraine is an associate partner of the international INORMS network
INORMS.part1: PNRM Ukraine is an associate partner of the international INORMS network.
INORMS.part2: This partnership was born from a deep belief that the profession can be not only technical, but human. Not only efficient, but emotionally meaningful. Not only local, but globally responsible.
INORMS.part3: In May 2025, during the annual INORMS meeting in Madrid, the Madrid Statement was presented — a strategic framework outlining five key principles for the sustainable development of the profession. We joined this initiative because it resonates with our core values: openness, care, inclusion, professional dignity, and shared responsibility.
INORMS.part4: We support research that holds social value — from inclusive reading initiatives to educational flashmobs. We strive to ensure that the voices of managers, coordinators, and curators are heard clearly and respectfully. We create conditions for professional growth, support emerging specialists, and open new directions — at the intersection of culture, education, digital innovation, and emotional literacy. Most importantly, we nurture a community where mutual support, knowledge exchange, and warm professional rituals have a place.
INORMS.part5: For us, partnership is not a formality. It is a space for shared breath, for exchanging meanings, for shaping a future where every child has access to a book, every professional to recognition, and every idea to realization.
SUSTED.title: SUSTED — Education for Sustainable Development
SUSTED.subtitle: An international project under the ERASMUS+ programme (KA2 CBHE) | PNRM Ukraine — Associated Partner
SUSTED.projectDescription: The project "Education for Sustainable Development: Competence Synergy for Ukraine's Recovery" (SUSTED) within the ERASMUS+ program under the KA2 "Higher Education Capacity Building" (CBHE) direction
SUSTED.partner1: The Hague University of Applied Sciences (Netherlands)
SUSTED.partner2: University of Economics and Innovation in Lublin (Poland)
SUSTED.partner7: Volodymyr Dahl East Ukrainian National University
SUSTED.associatedPartner: Professional Network of Education and Science Managers of Ukraine
SUSTED.projectGoal: The project's goal is defined as contributing to strengthening the capacity of higher education institutions to uphold resilience in post-war recovery of Ukraine through developing competencies for sustainable management and leadership.
SUSTED.task1: identify the role and assess the potential of universities in forming student competencies in sustainable development management;
SUSTED.task2: develop and implement a sustainable development management course for successful employment of graduates according to Ukraine's recovery needs;
SUSTED.task3: develop knowledge transfer mechanisms between HEIs through creating a Virtual SD-laboratory of sustainable actions;
SUSTED.task4: support cooperation between HEIs in the "business-university-community" triad and build their capacity to ensure sustainable growth.
welcome.title: Welcome to PNRM Ukraine!
welcome.description1: The Professional Network of Research and Higher Education Managers of Ukraine (PNRM Ukraine) is a vibrant community of experts shaping the future of Ukrainian science and education through strategic management, communications, and collaboration.
welcome.description2: We bring together research managers, coordinators, communicators, scholars, and institutional leaders working across universities, research institutes, government agencies, and civil society. Our mission is to foster professional growth, share best practices, and co-create innovative solutions for managing research and education in Ukraine.
welcome.description3: Each member of our network contributes a unique perspective—regional, institutional, and professional. We believe that diversity is our strength: it allows us to compare challenges, exchange successful strategies, and adapt global models to the Ukrainian context.
welcome.description4: PNRM Ukraine actively engages with international partners, including through participation in the INORMS — International Network of Research Management Societies. This global platform enables us to join cross-border dialogues, take part in joint training programs, exchange resources, and co-develop support tools for research and education managers.
welcome.secretariat: The INORMS Secretariat is currently hosted by EARMA (European Association of Research Managers and Administrators) from June 2025 to May 2027.
brandbook.title: PNRM Ukraine Brandbook
brandbook.description: Explore the visual standards and communication style of our organization.
gallery.title: Photo gallery
about.p1: The Professional Network of Research and Higher Education Managers of Ukraine (PNRM Ukraine) is an independent initiative created to support professionals working in the field of research and education management.
about.p2: It all began with a need — deep, conscious, almost intuitive. A need for the voices of those who work behind the scenes of Ukrainian science and education to finally resonate in harmony. Research managers, international project coordinators, communicators, administrators — those who are rarely in the spotlight, yet without whom no breakthrough is possible.
about.p3: In 2022, amid the challenges of war, transformation, and the search for new meaning, the idea of forming a professional network was born. Not just a platform for exchanging experience, but a space of trust, support, and shared growth. This is how the Professional Network of Research and Higher Education Managers of Ukraine (PNRM Ukraine) came into being.
about.p4: The initiative united people who believed that Ukrainian science must not only survive, but flourish. That management is not a technical function, but a strategic art. That communication is not merely a tool, but a way to shape new realities.
about.p5: The first meetings were small, almost intimate — online conversations, discussions of needs, dreams, and challenges. Then came the first events, campaigns, the website, and international connections. PNRM began to speak — in Ukraine, across Europe, and within global networks.
about.p6: Today, PNRM Ukraine is more than a network. It is a community shaping a new culture of research and education management. It is the voice of those who build the infrastructure of knowledge. It is a symbol that even in the darkest times, creation is possible — with love, with humor, and with faith in the future.
team.organization.head.title: Chairperson and Secretary of the Organization
team.organization.head.subtitle: The Chairperson provides strategic leadership, represents the organization in public and institutional settings, and ensures adherence to its mission and values.
team.table.name: Full Name
team.member.hanna.name: Hanna Tolstanova
team.member.hanna.achievements: Founding key areas of activity, forming the first team, launching initiatives in science and education
team.member.victoria.name: Victoriia Hura
team.member.victoria.achievements: Strengthened international cooperation and expanded the organizational network.
team.secretary.title: Secretary
team.secretary.subtitle: The Secretary of the Organization plays a key role in ensuring stable communication, document management, and internal coordination. Through attention to detail, clarity in execution, and a deep understanding of the Organization’s mission, the Secretary sustains its daily vitality.
team.member.shalimova.name: Nataliia Shalimova
team.member.shalimova.achievements: Implementation of an internal documentation system that ensures transparency and convenience across all organizational bodies. Coordination of Board and Supervisory Council meetings, including preparation of minutes, agendas, and resolutions. Facilitation of communication among Organization members, especially during active campaigns, leadership transitions, or strategic planning periods. Archiving of key decisions and documents, preserving the Organization’s developmental history and institutional memory. Contribution to reporting processes, including preparation of materials for public presentations, grant applications, and partner meetings.
team.supervisory.title: Supervisory Board
team.supervisory.subtitle: The Supervisory Board is a consultative and oversight body of the Organization. It monitors financial and operational activities, approves key documents, and ensures that assets are used in alignment with the Organization’s mission and goals. The Board is elected by the General Assembly and operates on a voluntary basis. Its members have an advisory vote at meetings of the Executive Board.
team.member.shevchuk.name:
Oksana Shevchuk

Chair of the Supervisory Board.
(Professor, Doctor of Medical Sciences, Professor of the Department of Pharmacology and Clinical Pharmacology, Head of the Scientific Project Management Department, I. Horbachevsky Ternopil National Medical University, Ternopil)
team.member.nesterenko.name:
Roman Nesterenko

Member of the Supervisory Board.
(Deputy Head of the International Relations Office, V. N. Karazin Kharkiv National University, Kharkiv)
team.member.nshalimova.name:
Nataliya Shalimova

Member of the Supervisory Board.
(Professor, Doctor of Economic Sciences, Dean of the Faculty of Economics, Central Ukrainian National Technical University, Kropyvnytskyi)
team.advisory.council.title: Advisory Council
team.advisory.council.subtitle: The Advisory Council is a collegial consultative body of the NGO "Professional Network of Education and Science Managers of Ukraine" (PNRM Ukraine). Its purpose is to provide expert and strategic support in the fields of education and science, contributing to the implementation of PNRM Ukraine's long-term goals. The Council operates in accordance with Ukrainian legislation, the Organization's Statute, and its internal regulations
team.member.freudenreich.achievements: Expert in higher education management and academic leadership development. Managing Director of the Potsdam Graduate School at the University of Potsdam, Germany. With extensive experience at leading research organizations including the Helmholtz Association and the Alexander von Humboldt Foundation.
team.member.butych.achievements: Regional Coordinator for Central and Eastern Europe at the International Office of Leibniz University Hannover, Germany. Since 2010, she has been developing and fostering international university collaboration with Eastern Europe. Managing Director of the German-Ukrainian Academic Society.
team.member.degtyarova.achievements: SGH Rector's Representative for Cooperation with Ukrainian Higher Education Institutions, SGH Warsaw School of Economics, Senior Researcher at Polish Rectors Foundation. In 2015-2020, she was Vice-Chairman of the Council of Young Scientists under the Ministry of Education and Science of Ukraine.
team.member.arnaout.achievements: Professor for International Management at Hochschule Wismar, University of Applied Sciences, Germany, where he heads the English-language Master's program in International Management. Plays a leading role in Hochschule Wismar's cooperation with Ukraine through the EUComplianceM4UA Erasmus+ project.
